creator: riggs@FreeBSD.org
text:
Yep, poudriere is the tool that will help you save the day many times. A step by step tutorial is included in the porter's handbook:
https://www.freebsd.org/doc/en/books/porters-handbook/testing-poudriere.html

By the way, since poudriere runs stage-qa automatically during a testport run, it would also have told you that you heed USES=shebangfix in gvp's Makefile :-)

No worries, though. Since it was a minimal change, I took the liberty of fixing it before committing.

Continue to have fun with Go and take care of your ports :)
